---
name: Tweak
about: Propose a small adjustment to behaviour that already works — spacing, copy, a default, an ordering. No new capability. A maintainer must agree this issue before a PR is raised.
title: "[Tweak] "
labels: ["tweak", "needs-agreement"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
A Tweak adjusts something that already works and adds no new ability. Copy,
spacing, colour, ordering, a default value, a keybinding. If the change were
never made, nobody would say throng was MISSING anything — only that it was
slightly worse.

If it gives a user a new ability, raise an Enhancement instead.
If the current behaviour contradicts its own stated intent, that is not a
Tweak — it is a Bug, so raise a Bug report.

A maintainer must AGREE this issue before a PR is opened. Tweaks are usually
small enough to skip the full Spec Kit lifecycle, but they still need an
agreed issue.
-->

## Current behaviour

<!-- What does throng do today? Name the surface (and the file/rule if you know it). -->

## Proposed adjustment

<!-- What should it do instead? Be specific — "a bit more padding" is harder to close than
     "14px → 16px horizontal padding on the tab chip". -->

## Why this is a Tweak, not a Bug

<!-- Confirm the current behaviour works as designed and the design is simply weaker than it
     could be. If it is actually broken, close this and raise a Bug report. -->

## Acceptance criteria (testable)

<!-- The observable conditions that mean this is done. Include what must NOT change —
     a Tweak that quietly shifts the surrounding layout is a regression. -->

- [ ]
- [ ]

## Maintainer agreement

<!-- Leave blank — a maintainer fills this in. -->
- [ ] Agreed by maintainer. Contributor may proceed (full lifecycle unless trivially small).
